title: Executive Order 12232—Historically Black Colleges and Universities

flags.justification: The Secretary of Education coordinates, but each agency retains its own implementation responsibility — a distributed model.
Sec. 1-108 requires an annual report to the President including measurable participation levels by agency — a specific, concrete accountability mechanism.
Multiple related sub-requirements (goal-setting, program review, barrier identification) all coherently serve one purpose.
This is an aspirational barrier-identification and improvement initiative, not a compliance regime with defined violations or penalties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
No review or reauthorization mechanism is specified for this standing, ongoing equity-improvement initiative.
This creates new review and reporting requirements rather than removing any existing oversight.

Sec. 1-107(b) explicitly gives each president of a historically Black college or university the opportunity to comment on implementation — a direct stakeholder-input mechanism for the affected institutions.
Establishes a durable, government-wide equity-improvement initiative, part of a lineage of similar orders continued under later administrations.
A coordination model with no competing mandate.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
